questions.content: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about eating wild fungi?
options: A Cooking doesn’t make poisonous fungi edible.
B Edible wild fungi can be eaten without cooking.
C Wild fungi are highly nutritious.
D Some edible fungi look very similar to poisonous varieties.
E Fungi which cannot be identified should only be eaten in small quantities.

sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 26-30: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G next to questions 26-30.
sections.passage_or_context: Research project on attitudes towards study

questions.content: Phoebe’s main reason for choosing her topic was that
options: A her classmates had been very interested in it.
B it would help prepare her for her first teaching post.
C she had been inspired by a particular book.

questions.content: Phoebe’s main research question related to
options: A the effect of teacher discipline.
B the variety of learning activities.

questions.content: Phoebe was most surprised by her finding that
options: A gender did not influence behaviour significantly.
B girls were more negative about school than boys.
C boys were more talkative than girls in class.

questions.content: Regarding teaching, Phoebe says she has learned that
options: A teachers should be flexible in their lesson planning.
B brighter children learn from supporting weaker ones.
C children vary from each other in unpredictable ways.

questions.content: Tony is particularly impressed by Phoebe’s ability to
options: A recognise the limitations of such small-scale research.
B reflect on her own research experience in an interesting way.
C design her research in such a way as to minimise difficulties.
